title: Knee pain
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports usage-related joint pain, stiffness, motion restriction, tenderness, swelling, instability, functional limitation, night pain, fever, redness or heat, weight loss, and systemic concern features.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped usage-related pain, stiffness, swelling, range, tenderness, gait, fever, redness, warmth, night symptoms, and functional impact in OA-compatible presentations.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports usage-related joint pain, stiffness, motion restriction, tenderness, swelling, instability, functional limitation, night pain, fever, redness or heat, weight loss, and systemic concern features.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Osteoarthritis – 28

investigations.text: Defines clinical diagnosis in typical adults, cautions that radiographic findings may not match symptoms, and limits MRI or other imaging to selected uncertainty or alternative-diagnosis contexts.
investigations.rationale: Supports OA terminology and selected X-ray or MRI review without inferring diagnosis from imaging.
investigations.evidence_extract: Defines clinical diagnosis in typical adults, cautions that radiographic findings may not match symptoms, and limits MRI or other imaging to selected uncertainty or alternative-diagnosis contexts.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Osteoarthritis – 28

investigations.text: Defines concern and specialist contexts including a hot swollen joint, persistent fever, night symptoms, weight loss, face-to-face assessment needs, worsening symptoms, severe functional limitation, and selected imaging.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped hot-joint concern, worsening, severe-function, return, imaging, and clinician-arranged follow-up documentation.
investigations.evidence_extract: Defines concern and specialist contexts including a hot swollen joint, persistent fever, night symptoms, weight loss, face-to-face assessment needs, worsening symptoms, severe functional limitation, and selected imaging.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Osteoarthritis – 28

assessment.text: Describes knee pain location, stairs-related pain, giving way, instability, and hip groin or thigh pain, stiffness, walking pain, restricted movement, and early loss of internal rotation.
assessment.rationale: Directly supports mapped knee pain, stairs-related pain, instability or giving-way, and function fields.
assessment.evidence_extract: Describes knee pain location, stairs-related pain, giving way, instability, and hip groin or thigh pain, stiffness, walking pain, restricted movement, and early loss of internal rotation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Osteoarthritis – 28

source_limitations: Acute trauma, inability to bear weight, locking, clicking, ligament or meniscal tests, open wounds, neurovascular symptoms, RICE, medication, and fixed follow-up remain unsupported.
The osteoarthritis label must remain clinician-stated and age/context dependent.

curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
